question: How does the distribution of Numerical Aptitude scores differ across various Career groupings?

analysis.chart_type: Violin Heatmap of Median Numerical Aptitude for Top 10 Most Frequent Careers
analysis.design_rationale: Given that most careers are unique, we focus on the top 10 most common career titles by frequency, showing a heatmap that encodes the median Numerical Aptitude score for each. To reveal both centrality and spread, we also show violin plots (mirrored KDEs) horizontally per career. Combining a violin plot with a heatmap emphasis (cell color indicating the median) gives users a dual insight: both the typical range as a density curve and an intuitive color readout of the group's central tendency.
analysis.target_insight: This hybrid chart shows, for the most popular career choices, which have the widest/narrowest variation in quantitative aptitude, while the cell coloring provides an at-a-glance view of which careers tend to attract high or low scorers.

chart_encodings.y: Top 10 most frequent Career (ordered by median Numerical Aptitude)
chart_encodings.color: Cell background: heatmap color by median Numerical Aptitude per Career
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Horizontal violin plots, career labels y-axis, colormap 'viridis'

transformations: Identify the 10 most frequent careers using value_counts on df['Career'].
Subset the dataframe to only those careers.
Compute the median 'Numerical Aptitude' for each of those careers.
Order the careers by median Numerical Aptitude (descending).
data_preparation_steps: Compute df['Career'].value_counts().nlargest(10).index to get top 10 carrier titles.
Subset df to only rows where Career is in that top 10 list.
For ordering, group by Career, compute median 'Numerical Aptitude', order accordingly.
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
import seaborn as sns

df = pd.read_csv("csv_c2_sec\12.csv")
# Top 10 most frequent career titles
career_counts = df["Career"].value_counts()
top10_careers = career_counts.nlargest(10).index.tolist()
df_top10 = df[df["Career"].isin(top10_careers)].copy()
# Order by median Numerical Aptitude descending
medians = df_top10.groupby("Career")["Numerical Aptitude"].median().sort_values(ascending=False)
order = medians.index.tolist()
# Normalize medians for coloring
min_med, max_med = medians.min(), medians.max()
norm = mpl.colors.Normalize(vmin=min_med, vmax=max_med)
cmap = mpl.colormaps["viridis"]
plt.figure(figsize=(11,7))
ax = plt.gca()
for i, career in enumerate(order):
    yloc = i
    scores = df_top10[df_top10["Career"] == career]["Numerical Aptitude"].values
    parts = ax.violinplot(scores, positions=[yloc], vert=False, widths=0.76, showmeans=False, showmedians=False, showextrema=False)
    color = cmap(norm(medians[career]))
    for pc in parts['bodies']:
        pc.set_facecolor(color)
        pc.set_edgecolor("k")
        pc.set_alpha(0.76)
    # Overlay the median as a dot
    median = np.median(scores)
    ax.plot(median, yloc, marker="o", color="#222", markersize=7, zorder=3)
# Format axes
ax.set_yticks(range(len(order)))
ax.set_yticklabels(order, fontsize=11)
ax.set_xlabel("Numerical Aptitude Score", fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel("Career (Top 10 by Frequency)", fontsize=13)
plt.title("Numerical Aptitude Distribution for\nMost Frequent Careers (Violin Heatmap)", fontsize=15, fontweight="bold")
# Create a colorbar showing median mapping
sm = mpl.cm.ScalarMappable(norm=norm, cmap=cmap)
cbar = plt.colorbar(sm, ax=ax, orientation="vertical", pad=0.02)
cbar.set_label("Median Numerical Aptitude", fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
